title: TC011-View dashboard status and profile details
description: Verifies that an authenticated user can open the dashboard and see their authorization status and profile information.
code:
import asyncio
import re
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",
                "--ipc=host",
                "--single-process"
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        # Wider default timeout to match the agent's DOM-stability budget;
        # auto-waiting Playwright APIs (expect, locator.wait_for) inherit this.
        context.set_default_timeout(15000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> navigate
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=5000)
        except Exception:
            pass
        
        # -> Input the volunteer email into element 6, input the volunteer password into element 7, then click the Entrar button at element 47 to attempt login and open the dashboard.
        # email input
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/div[2]/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("demo.voluntario@test.local")
        
        # -> Input the volunteer email into element 6, input the volunteer password into element 7, then click the Entrar button at element 47 to attempt login and open the dashboard.
        # password input
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/div[3]/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("DemoPassword123!")
        
        # -> Input the volunteer email into element 6, input the volunteer password into element 7, then click the Entrar button at element 47 to attempt login and open the dashboard.
        # button "Entrar"
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/button").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC015-Open QR scan access from the dashboard
description: Verifies that an authorized user can open the QR scan area from the dashboard when access is allowed.
code:
import asyncio
import re
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",
                "--ipc=host",
                "--single-process"
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        # Wider default timeout to match the agent's DOM-stability budget;
        # auto-waiting Playwright APIs (expect, locator.wait_for) inherit this.
        context.set_default_timeout(15000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> navigate
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=5000)
        except Exception:
            pass
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields with the volunteer credentials and submit the form by clicking the Entrar button.
        # email input
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/div[2]/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("demo.voluntario@test.local")
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields with the volunteer credentials and submit the form by clicking the Entrar button.
        # password input
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/div[3]/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("DemoPassword123!")
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields with the volunteer credentials and submit the form by clicking the Entrar button.
        # button "Entrar"
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/button").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Registrar entrada o salida' control (anchor index 103) to open the QR access area and verify the QR scan/manual-mode controls are displayed.
        # link "Registrar entrada o salida"
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/div/section/button/a").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC019-Search users by name, email, and role
description: An admin can search for users and filter the results by role to find matching records with their status and registration date.
code:
import asyncio
import re
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",
                "--ipc=host",
                "--single-process"
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        # Wider default timeout to match the agent's DOM-stability budget;
        # auto-waiting Playwright APIs (expect, locator.wait_for) inherit this.
        context.set_default_timeout(15000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> navigate
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=5000)
        except Exception:
            pass
        
        # -> Create todo.md checklist, then input the admin email and password into indexes 7 and 8 and click the Entrar button (index 9) to submit the login form.
        # email input
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/div[2]/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("admin@test.local")
        
        # -> Create todo.md checklist, then input the admin email and password into indexes 7 and 8 and click the Entrar button (index 9) to submit the login form.
        # password input
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/div[3]/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("AdminTestPassword123!")
        
        # -> Create todo.md checklist, then input the admin email and password into indexes 7 and 8 and click the Entrar button (index 9) to submit the login form.
        # button "Entrar"
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/button").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Usuarios' button (index 90) to open the user search view so users can be searched and filtered.
        # button "Usuarios"
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/nav/button[3]").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.click()
        
        # -> Enter 'demo.personal@test.local' into the search field (index 170) and wait for the results to update so the result row can be verified.
        # text input placeholder="Nombre, apellidos o correo"
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section/div/div/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("demo.personal@test.local")
        
        # -> Replace the search input with 'demo.voluntario@test.local', wait for results to update, and then verify that the matching row displays role, status, and registration date.
        # text input placeholder="Nombre, apellidos o correo"
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section/div/div/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("demo.voluntario@test.local")
        
        # -> Click the 'Categoria' dropdown (element index 174) to open options so 'Voluntarios' can be selected and the results verified.
        # "Todas Voluntarios Personal Servicio soci..."
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section/div/div[2]/select").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC022-View the admin QR display
description: An admin can open the QR display view and see the current QR code with its expiration time.
code:
import asyncio
import re
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",
                "--ipc=host",
                "--single-process"
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        # Wider default timeout to match the agent's DOM-stability budget;
        # auto-waiting Playwright APIs (expect, locator.wait_for) inherit this.
        context.set_default_timeout(15000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> navigate
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        try:
            await page.wait_for_load_state("domcontentloaded", timeout=5000)
        except Exception:
            pass
        
        # -> Fill the admin email and password fields, then submit the login form by clicking the 'Entrar' button.
        # email input
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/div[2]/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("admin@test.local")
        
        # -> Fill the admin email and password fields, then submit the login form by clicking the 'Entrar' button.
        # password input
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/div[3]/input").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.fill("AdminTestPassword123!")
        
        # -> Fill the admin email and password fields, then submit the login form by clicking the 'Entrar' button.
        # button "Entrar"
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/section/div[2]/form/button").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'QR' button (interactive element index 93) to open the QR display view so the page can be inspected for the QR code and expiration time.
        # button "QR"
        elem = page.locator("xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/nav/button[6]").nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state="visible", timeout=10000)
        await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
